title: The Rise of the 15-Minute City
excerpt: Urban planners are redesigning metropolises to ensure everything residents need is a short walk away.
content:
The concept of the '15-Minute City' is transitioning from urban design theory to concrete reality. Several major cities have launched massive rezoning and infrastructure projects aimed at decentralizing city centers and creating vibrant, self-sufficient neighborhoods.

The core idea is simple: every citizen should be able to access work, school, healthcare, and leisure within a 15-minute walk or bike ride from their home. This involves a radical reduction in car reliance, an expansion of green spaces, and rethinking single-use commercial zoning.

Early adopters report significant improvements in air quality and community cohesion. However, challenges remain, particularly regarding housing affordability and ensuring that these hyper-local models do not inadvertently increase socioeconomic segregation.
author: Marc Dubois
